info.title: REST API for Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM - Salaries
info.description:
Last Updated January 2026

You can use Oracle REST APIs to view and manage data stored in Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM.

Whether you're experienced with or new to REST, use this guide to find what you need, including:

  • A quick start how-to that walks you through a simple request example.
  • Use cases that provide real-world solutions with code examples.
  • Detailed descriptions for each REST resource.
info.x-summary: Last Updated January 2026 You can use Oracle REST APIs to view and manage data stored in Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM.

post.summary: Finds the properties of the assignment.
post.description: Finds the properties of the assignment, such as progression ladder, grade, step, and FTE.

parameters.name: REST-Framework-Version
parameters.description: The protocol version between a REST client and service. If the client does not specify this header in the request the server will pick a default version for the API.

parameters.name: Metadata-Context
parameters.description: If the REST API supports runtime customizations, the shape of the service may change during runtime. The REST client may isolate itself from these changes or choose to interact with the latest version of the API by specifying this header. For example: Metadata-Context:sandbox="TrackEmployeeFeature".

requestBody.description: The following table describes the body parameters in the request for this task.

flowMap.description: Map that contains various details of the assignment.

assignmentId.description: Unique identifier for a worker assignment.

flowName.description: The name of the flow that's using the REST service.

effectiveDate.description: Date used to find the salary applicable for the day.

default.description: The following table describes the default response for this task.

get.summary: Get all salaries for a worker's assignment

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description: When this parameter is provided, the specified children are included in the resource payload (instead of just a link). The value of this query parameter is "all" or "". More than one child can be specified using comma as a separator. Example: ?expand=Employees,Localizations. Nested children can also be provided following the format "Child.NestedChild" (Example: ?expand=Employees.Managers). If a nested child is provided (Example: Employees.Managers), the missing children will be processed implicitly. For example, "?expand=Employees.Managers" is the same as "?expand=Employees,Employees.Managers" (which will expand Employees and Managers).

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description:
This parameter filters the resource fields. Only the specified fields are returned, which means that if no fields are specified, no fields are returned (useful to get only the links). If an indirect child resource is provided (Example: Employees.Managers), the missing children will be processed implicitly. For example, "?fields=Employees.Managers:Empname" is the same as "?fields=;Employees:;Employees.Managers:Empname" (which will only return the "Empname" field for Managers). the value of this query parameter is a list of resource fields. The attribute can be a direct (Example: Employees) or indirect (Example: Employees.Managers) child. It cannot be combined with expand query parameter. If both are provided, only fields will be considered.

Format: ?fields=Attribute1,Attribute2

Format for fields in child resource: ?fields=Accessor1:Attribute1,Attribute2

parameters.name: onlyData
parameters.description: The resource item payload will be filtered in order to contain only data (no links section, for example).

parameters.name: links
parameters.description:
This parameter can be used to show only certain links while accessing a singular resource or a resource collection. The parameter value format is a comma-separated list of : <link_relation>

Example:
self,canonical

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: This parameter restricts the number of resources returned inside the resource collection. If the limit exceeds the resource count then the framework will only return the available resources.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Used to define the starting position of the resource collection. If offset exceeds the resource count then no resources are returned. Default value is 0.

parameters.name: totalResults
parameters.description: The resource collection representation will include the "estimated row count" when "?totalResults=true", otherwise the count is not included.  The default value is "false".

parameters.name: q
parameters.description:
This query parameter defines the where clause. The resource collection will be queried using the provided expressions. The value of this query parameter is one or more expressions. Example: ?q=Deptno>=10 and <= 30;Loc!=NY

Format: ?q=expression1;expression2

You can use these queryable attributes to filter this collection resource using the q query parameter:
  • ActionCode; string; Code of the action.
  • ActionId; integer; Unique identifier of the action.
  • ActionName; string; Action being done on this assignment, such as, Hire, Add Pending Worker, Manager Change and so on. Valid values are defined in the actionsLOV.
  • ActionOccurrenceId; integer; Unique identifier of the action occurrence.
  • ActionReason; string; Reason for the action being done on the record. Valid values are defined in the actionReasonsLOV.
  • ActionReasonCode; string; Code of the action reason.
  • ActionReasonId; integer; Unique identifier of the action reason.
  • AdjustedAnnualFtSalary; number; Annual value of the worker's salary, when working full time per adjusted full-time equivalent.
  • AdjustedAnnualSalary; number; Annual value of the worker's salary, when working per adjusted full-time equivalent.
  • AdjustedFTEUsage; string; Indicates that adjusted FTE is used.
  • AdjustedFTEValue; number; Factor that represents the proportion of adjusted full-time hours a person works.
  • AdjustmentAmount; number; Calculated adjustment amount based on the previous salary.
  • AdjustmentPercentage; number; Calculated adjustment percentage based on the previous salary .
  • AmountDecimalPrecision; number; Decimal precision of the salary amount, configured in salary basis.
  • AmountRoundingCode; string; Code for the salary amount rounding rule.
  • AnnualFullTimeSalary; number; Annual value of the worker's salary, if working full time.
  • AnnualRoundingCode; string; Code for the annualized salary amount rounding rule.
  • AnnualSalary; number; Annual value of the worker's salary.
  • AssignmentId; integer; Unique identifier for the assignment.
  • AssignmentNumber; string; Assignment number for the given assignment.
  • AssignmentType; string; Type of assignment, such as an employee, contingent worker, nonworker, or pending worker.
  • BusinessTitle; string; Business title for the given assignment.
  • Code; string; Code for the salary basis.
  • CompaRatio; number; Salary as a percentage of the grade range midpoint. Salary at the midpoint is 100.
  • ComponentUsage; string; Property used to specify whether the incremental components type is SELECTED_COMPONENTS or USER_DECIDES_USAGE.
  • CreatedBy; string; Identifies the user who created the row.
  • CreationDate; string; Identifies the date and time that the row was created.
  • CurrencyCode; string; Currency code that the salary amount is stored in, such as USD.
  • DateFrom; string; Start date of the salary.
  • DateTo; string; End date of the salary.
  • DisplayName; string; Unique name for the set of details linked to a worker's base pay. These details include the payroll characteristics used to pay base earnings, the duration of the quoted base pay, and the factor used to annualize base pay. The details also include any components used to connect base pay adjustments to different reasons and any associated grade rate for salary validation.
  • FTEValue; number; Factor that represents the proportion of full-time hours a person works.
  • FrequencyName; string; Frequency of the salary basis, such as Annually, Monthly, Weekly, or Hourly.
  • GeographyName; string; Geographical zone used in compensation to calculate salary ranges.
  • GeographyTypeName; string; Geographical zone type used in compensation to calculate salary ranges.
  • GradeCode; string; Code for the current grade.
  • GradeId; integer; Unique identifier for the assignment grade.
  • GradeLadderName; string; Name of the grade ladder associated with the person's assignment.
  • GradeName; string; Name of the step associated with the person's assignment.
  • GradeStepName; string; Name of the grade associated with the person's assignment.
  • LastUpdateDate; string; Identifies the date and time that the row was most recently updated.
  • LastUpdatedBy; string; Identifies the user who most recently updated the row.
  • LegalEmployerName; string; Legal employer for the person's assignment.
  • LegislativeDataGroupId; integer; Unique identifier for the legislative data group.
  • MultipleComponents; string; Indicates whether the given salary record has any child records that are either Components, Simple Components, or Rates.
  • NextSalReviewDate; string; Date to review a person's assignment salary.
  • PayrollFactor; number; Payroll multiplier used to convert the salary amount to an annual salary amount, when the frequency is chosen as payroll period.
  • PayrollFrequencyCode; string; Code for the payroll frequency when the frequency is chosen as payroll period.
  • PendingTransactionExists; string; Indicates if a salary change for the person assignment is pending approval.
  • PersonDisplayName; string; Display name of the person.
  • PersonId; integer; Person identifier for the worker.
  • PersonNumber; string; Number of the person.
  • Quartile; string; Quartile containing the worker's salary. The salary range for the worker's grade is divided into four equal parts called quartiles.
  • QuartileMeaning; string; Lookup code meaning of the quartile containing the worker's salary.
  • Quintile; string; Quintile containing the worker's salary. The salary range for the worker's grade is divided into five equal parts called quintiles.
  • QuintileMeaning; string; Lookup code meaning of the quintile containing the worker's salary.
  • RangeErrorWarning; string; Indicates the salary range violation behavior for the salary basis, such as error, warning, or none.
  • RangePosition; number; Position of the worker's salary within the assignment grade salary range, expressed as a percentage of 100.
  • RangeRoundingCode; string; Code for the salary range rounding rule.
  • SalaryAmount; number; Salary amount on the salary record for the worker assignment.
  • SalaryAmountScale; integer; Decimal precision of the salary amount, configured in salary basis.
  • SalaryBasisId; integer; Unique identifier for the salary basis.
  • SalaryBasisName; string; Name of the salary basis.
  • SalaryBasisType; string; Type of the salary basis, such as Salary amount determined by the user or Components.
  • SalaryFactor; number; Multiplier that converts the salary amount to an annual salary amount. For example, if a worker's base pay is quoted hourly, the annualization factor is 2080 (40 hours per week x 52 weeks).
  • SalaryFrequencyCode; string; Code of the salary basis frequency, such as ANNUAL, MONTHLY, HOURLY.
  • SalaryId; integer; Unique identifier for a salary record generated by the application.
  • SalaryRangeDefaultValue; number; Value based on the worker assignment grade and grade rate attached with the salary basis.
  • SalaryRangeMaximum; number; Salary range maximum based on the worker assignment grade.
  • SalaryRangeMidPoint; number; Salary range midpoint based on the worker assignment grade.
  • SalaryRangeMinimum; number; Salary range minimum based on the worker assignment grade.
  • SalaryTransactionStatus; string; Indicate whether the salary in cache is new or a correction. The approver scenario in the salary history action uses this information during edits.
  • SearchDate; string; Date used to find the applicable salary for the day.
  • WorkAtHome; string; Indicates whether the worker is working from home or not.
  • hasFutureSalary; string; Indicates whether a future salary exists.

parameters.name: orderBy
parameters.description: This parameter orders a resource collection based on the specified fields.  The parameter value is a comma-separated string of attribute names, each optionally followed by a colon and "asc" or "desc".  Specify "asc" for ascending and "desc" for descending. The default value is "asc".  For example, ?orderBy=field1:asc,field2:desc

parameters.name: finder
parameters.description:
Used as a predefined finder to search the collection.

Format ?finder=<finderName>;<variableName>=<variableValue>,<variableName2>=<variableValue2>

The following are the available finder names and corresponding finder variables

  • PrimaryKey Finds the salary that matches the specified primary key criteria.
    Finder Variables
    • SalaryId; integer; Unique identifier of the salary for a worker assignment.
  • findAssignmentSalaryHistory Finds all salaries for the assignment that matches the specified criteria.
    Finder Variables
    • AssignmentId; integer; Unique identifier for a worker assignment.
    • EffectiveStartDate; string; Date used to find the salary applicable for the day.
  • findByAssignmentId Finds all salaries that match the specified criteria.
    Finder Variables
    • AssignmentId; integer; Unique identifier of a worker assignment.
  • findByAssignmentIdAndDate Finds all salaries that match the specified criteria.
    Finder Variables
    • AssignmentId; integer; Unique identifier of a worker assignment.
    • EffectiveDate; string; Date used to find the salary applicable for the day.
  • findByAssignmentIdAndEffectivePeriod Finds all salaries that match the specified criteria.
    Finder Variables
    • AssignmentId; integer; Unique identifier for a worker assignment.
    • DateFrom; string; Start date of the salary.
    • DateTo; string; End date of the salary.
  • findByCoreHrParams Finds the salary that matches the specified criteria.
    Finder Variables
    • AssigGradeLadderId; integer; Surrogate identifier for the current grade ladder.
    • AssigGradeStepId; integer; Surrogate identifier of the current assignment grade step.
    • AssignmentId; integer; Unique identifier for the assignment.
    • BusinessUnitId; integer; Unique identifier for the business unit.
    • DateFrom; string; Start date of the salary.
    • DateTo; string; End date of the salary.
    • FTEValue; number; Factor that represents the proportion of full-time hours a person works.
    • GradeId; integer; Unique identifier for the assignment grade.
    • JobId; integer; Unique identifier of the job.
    • LocationId; integer; Unique identifier for the location.
    • RestFlow; string; The action or flow using the REST operation, such as for Hire, Promote, or Local and Global Transfer.
    • SalaryAmount; number; Salary amount on the salary record for the worker assignment.
    • SalaryBasisId; integer; Unique identifier of the salary basis.
    • SalaryId; integer; Unique identifier for the salary.
    • WorkAtHome; string; Lookup code to indicate work-from-home status of a remote worker.
  • findBySalaryId Finds the salary that matches the specified criteria.
    Finder Variables
    • SalaryId; integer; Unique identifier of the salary for a worker assignment.
  • findOfferSalaryView Finds all salaries for the assignment that matches the specified criteria.
    Finder Variables
    • AssignmentId; integer; Unique identifier for a worker assignment.
    • OfferId; integer; Unique identifier for an offer.
    • PersonId; integer; Unique identifier for the person.
  • findSalaryAfterReload Finds the salary that matches the specified criteria.
    Finder Variables
    • AssignmentId; integer; Unique identifier for a worker assignment.
    • ReloadTransactionId; integer; Unique identifier for the salary transaction.
    • SearchDate; string; Date used to find the salary applicable for the day.
  • findSalaryHistoryAfterReload Finds all salaries for the assignment that matches the specified criteria.
    Finder Variables
    • AssignmentId; integer; Unique identifier for a worker assignment.
    • ReloadTransactionId; integer; Unique identifier for the salary transaction.

ReloadTransactionId.description: Unique identifier for the salary transaction.

AssignmentId.description: Unique identifier for a worker assignment.

parameters.name: REST-Framework-Version
parameters.description: The protocol version between a REST client and service. If the client does not specify this header in the request the server will pick a default version for the API.

parameters.name: Metadata-Context
parameters.description: If the REST API supports runtime customizations, the shape of the service may change during runtime. The REST client may isolate itself from these changes or choose to interact with the latest version of the API by specifying this header. For example: Metadata-Context:sandbox="TrackEmployeeFeature".

default.description: The following table describes the default response for this task.

getTxnCacheSalary.description: Gets the current and proposed salary details from the transaction cache.

postOfferSalaryRow.description: Posts salary details for the offer assignment.

switchSalaryRow.description: Finds the properties of the proposed salary basis, such as grade rate, differential profile, progression ladder, and rate values.

getAssignmentInfo.description: Finds the properties of the assignment, such as progression ladder, grade, step, and FTE.

whenAndWhyRowValidation.description: Validate the when and why section details, such as effective date, action, and reason, after the person clicks Continue.

setSalaryHistoryCache.description: Initializes HCM Parameters, such as Person Identifier and Assignment Identifier, for salary history Business Intelligence Publisher notification.

getSrdTesterRecalculatedValues.description: Finds the worker's recalculated values, such as grade and location, based on changes on the simulate salary range page.

coreHrSalaryRowValidation.description: Validates the data when the person clicks Continue in flows, such as Change Salary.

initializeSRDTester.description: Finds the worker's initial values, such as grade, location, and salary basis, on the simulate salary range page.

coreHrSalaryRowValidationDFF.description: Validates the data that uses descriptive flexfields after the person clicks Continue in various flows, such as Change Salary.

getOfferSalaryRow.description: Gets salary details from the offer assignment.

getAutoCompleteRefData.description: Gets the assignment attributes to use in business rules that default and validate salary attributes in the Change Salary action.

getSimpleCalculatedAttrs.description: Gets all the simple components used in calculations for another simple component.

post.summary: Create a salary for a worker's assignment

parameters.name: Upsert-Mode
parameters.description: Contains one of the following values: true or false. If true, the server performs an Upsert operation instead of a Create operation. During an Upsert operation, the server attempts to find an existing resource that matches the payload. If a match is found, the server updates the existing resource instead of creating a new one. If not found or false (default), the server performs a Create operation. Note that the Upsert operation isn't supported for date-effective REST resources.

get.summary: Get a salary for a worker's assignment

parameters.name: SalaryId
parameters.description: The value of this parameter could be a hash of the key that is used to uniquely identify the resource item. The client should not generate the hash key value.  Instead, the client should query on the collection resource with a filter to navigate to a specific resource item. For example: products?q=InventoryItemId=<value1>

schema.description: Unique identifier for a salary record generated by the application.

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description: When this parameter is provided, the specified children are included in the resource payload (instead of just a link). The value of this query parameter is "all" or "". More than one child can be specified using comma as a separator. Example: ?expand=Employees,Localizations. Nested children can also be provided following the format "Child.NestedChild" (Example: ?expand=Employees.Managers). If a nested child is provided (Example: Employees.Managers), the missing children will be processed implicitly. For example, "?expand=Employees.Managers" is the same as "?expand=Employees,Employees.Managers" (which will expand Employees and Managers).

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description:
This parameter filters the resource fields. Only the specified fields are returned, which means that if no fields are specified, no fields are returned (useful to get only the links). If an indirect child resource is provided (Example: Employees.Managers), the missing children will be processed implicitly. For example, "?fields=Employees.Managers:Empname" is the same as "?fields=;Employees:;Employees.Managers:Empname" (which will only return the "Empname" field for Managers). the value of this query parameter is a list of resource fields. The attribute can be a direct (Example: Employees) or indirect (Example: Employees.Managers) child. It cannot be combined with expand query parameter. If both are provided, only fields will be considered.

Format: ?fields=Attribute1,Attribute2

Format for fields in child resource: ?fields=Accessor1:Attribute1,Attribute2

parameters.name: onlyData
parameters.description: The resource item payload will be filtered in order to contain only data (no links section, for example).

parameters.name: dependency
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies dependencies which are fields that are set before and rolled back after generating the response. Generally they are used to preview the effects of an attribute change. The fields specified in this parameter are always set in the resource instance in question. When a child resource collection is requested and the parameter is set, the fields will be set in the parent resource instance before generating the resource collection payload. The value of this query parameter is a set of dependency fields. Example: dependency=ProductId=2

Format: <attr1>=<val1>,<attr2>=<value2>

parameters.name: links
parameters.description:
This parameter can be used to show only certain links while accessing a singular resource or a resource collection. The parameter value format is a comma-separated list of : <link_relation>

Example:
self,canonical

requestBody.description: The following table describes the body parameters in the request for this task.

inputParameterMap.description: Map that contains various details of the input parameters.

actionOccurrenceMap.description: Map that contains details of the action occurrence.

assignmentModifiedAttributes.description: Map that has the changed details for the assignment.

salaryMap.description: Map that contains details of the salary.

salaryChildMap.description: Map that contains details of the salary components.

hcmParamsMap.description: Map that contains details of the HCM parameters.

operation.description: Type of operation being performed such as Create, Correct.

default.description: The following table describes the default response for this task.

count.description: The number of resource instances returned in the current range.

hasMore.description: Indicates whether more resources are available on the server than the subset returned in the response. If the value is true, then there are more resources to retrieve from the server. The default value is false.

limit.description: The actual paging size used by the server.

links.title: Links
links.description: The link relations associated with the resource instance.

offset.description: The offset value used in the current page.

totalResults.description: The estimated row count when "?totalResults=true", otherwise the count is not included.

ActionName.title: Action
ActionName.description: Action being done on this assignment, such as, Hire, Add Pending Worker, Manager Change and so on. Valid values are defined in the actionsLOV.

ActionOccurrenceId.description: Unique identifier of the action occurrence.

ActionReason.title: Action Reason
ActionReason.description: Reason for the action being done on the record. Valid values are defined in the actionReasonsLOV.

ActionReasonId.title: Action Reason
ActionReasonId.description: Unique identifier of the action reason.

AdjustedAnnualFtSalary.description: Annual value of the worker's salary, when working full time per adjusted full-time equivalent.

AdjustedAnnualSalary.description: Annual value of the worker's salary, when working per adjusted full-time equivalent.

AdjustedFTEUsage.title: Adjusted FTE
AdjustedFTEUsage.description: Indicates that adjusted FTE is used.

AdjustedFTEValue.description: Factor that represents the proportion of adjusted full-time hours a person works.

AdjustmentAmount.title: Adjustment Amount
AdjustmentAmount.description: Calculated adjustment amount based on the previous salary.

AdjustmentPercentage.title: Adjustment Percentage
AdjustmentPercentage.description: Calculated adjustment percentage based on the previous salary .

AmountDecimalPrecision.description: Decimal precision of the salary amount, configured in salary basis.

AmountRoundingCode.description: Code for the salary amount rounding rule.

AnnualFullTimeSalary.title: Annualized Full-Time Salary
AnnualFullTimeSalary.description: Annual value of the worker's salary, if working full time.

AnnualRoundingCode.description: Code for the annualized salary amount rounding rule.

AnnualSalary.title: Annual Salary
AnnualSalary.description: Annual value of the worker's salary.

AssignmentNumber.description: Assignment number for the given assignment.

AssignmentType.description: Type of assignment, such as an employee, contingent worker, nonworker, or pending worker.

BusinessTitle.description: Business title for the given assignment.

CompaRatio.title: Compa-Ratio
CompaRatio.description: Salary as a percentage of the grade range midpoint. Salary at the midpoint is 100.

ComponentUsage.description: Property used to specify whether the incremental components type is SELECTED_COMPONENTS or USER_DECIDES_USAGE.

CreatedBy.title: Created By
CreatedBy.description: Identifies the user who created the row.

CreationDate.title: Creation Date
CreationDate.description: Identifies the date and time that the row was created.

CurrencyCode.title: Currency
CurrencyCode.description: Currency code that the salary amount is stored in, such as USD.

DisplayName.title: Salary Basis
DisplayName.description: Unique name for the set of details linked to a worker's base pay. These details include the payroll characteristics used to pay base earnings, the duration of the quoted base pay, and the factor used to annualize base pay. The details also include any components used to connect base pay adjustments to different reasons and any associated grade rate for salary validation.

FTEValue.title: FTE
FTEValue.description: Factor that represents the proportion of full-time hours a person works.

FrequencyName.title: Frequency
FrequencyName.description: Frequency of the salary basis, such as Annually, Monthly, Weekly, or Hourly.

GeographyName.title: Compensation Zone
GeographyName.description: Geographical zone used in compensation to calculate salary ranges.

GeographyTypeName.title: Compensation Zone Type
GeographyTypeName.description: Geographical zone type used in compensation to calculate salary ranges.

GradeId.title: Grade
GradeId.description: Unique identifier for the assignment grade.

GradeLadderName.title: Grade Ladder
GradeLadderName.description: Name of the grade ladder associated with the person's assignment.

GradeName.title: Grade
GradeName.description: Name of the step associated with the person's assignment.

GradeStepName.title: Grade Step
GradeStepName.description: Name of the grade associated with the person's assignment.

LastUpdateDate.title: Last Update Date
LastUpdateDate.description: Identifies the date and time that the row was most recently updated.

LastUpdatedBy.title: Last Updated By
LastUpdatedBy.description: Identifies the user who most recently updated the row.

LegalEmployerName.title: Legal Employer
LegalEmployerName.description: Legal employer for the person's assignment.

LegislativeDataGroupId.description: Unique identifier for the legislative data group.

MultipleComponents.title: Use salary components
MultipleComponents.description: Indicates whether the given salary record has any child records that are either Components, Simple Components, or Rates.

NextSalReviewDate.title: Next Salary Review Date
NextSalReviewDate.description: Date to review a person's assignment salary.

PayrollFactor.description: Payroll multiplier used to convert the salary amount to an annual salary amount, when the frequency is chosen as payroll period.

PayrollFrequencyCode.title: Frequency
PayrollFrequencyCode.description: Code for the payroll frequency when the frequency is chosen as payroll period.

PendingTransactionExists.description: Indicates if a salary change for the person assignment is pending approval.

Quartile.title: Quartile
Quartile.description: Quartile containing the worker's salary. The salary range for the worker's grade is divided into four equal parts called quartiles.

QuartileMeaning.title: Quartile
QuartileMeaning.description: Lookup code meaning of the quartile containing the worker's salary.

Quintile.title: Quintile
Quintile.description: Quintile containing the worker's salary. The salary range for the worker's grade is divided into five equal parts called quintiles.

QuintileMeaning.title: Quintile
QuintileMeaning.description: Lookup code meaning of the quintile containing the worker's salary.

RangeErrorWarning.description: Indicates the salary range violation behavior for the salary basis, such as error, warning, or none.

RangePosition.title: Range Position
RangePosition.description: Position of the worker's salary within the assignment grade salary range, expressed as a percentage of 100.

RangeRoundingCode.description: Code for the salary range rounding rule.

SalaryAmount.title: Salary Amount
SalaryAmount.description: Salary amount on the salary record for the worker assignment.

SalaryAmountScale.description: Decimal precision of the salary amount, configured in salary basis.

SalaryBasisId.description: Unique identifier for the salary basis.

SalaryBasisType.description: Type of the salary basis, such as Salary amount determined by the user or Components.

SalaryFactor.title: Salary Annualization Factor
SalaryFactor.description: Multiplier that converts the salary amount to an annual salary amount. For example, if a worker's base pay is quoted hourly, the annualization factor is 2080 (40 hours per week x 52 weeks).

SalaryFrequencyCode.title: Frequency
SalaryFrequencyCode.description: Code of the salary basis frequency, such as ANNUAL, MONTHLY, HOURLY.

SalaryId.description: Unique identifier for a salary record generated by the application.

SalaryRangeDefaultValue.description: Value based on the worker assignment grade and grade rate attached with the salary basis.

SalaryRangeMaximum.title: Salary Range Maximum
SalaryRangeMaximum.description: Salary range maximum based on the worker assignment grade.

SalaryRangeMidPoint.title: Salary Range Midpoint
SalaryRangeMidPoint.description: Salary range midpoint based on the worker assignment grade.

SalaryRangeMinimum.title: Salary Range Minimum
SalaryRangeMinimum.description: Salary range minimum based on the worker assignment grade.

SalaryTransactionStatus.description: Indicate whether the salary in cache is new or a correction. The approver scenario in the salary history action uses this information during edits.

SearchDate.description: Date used to find the applicable salary for the day.

WorkAtHome.description: Indicates whether the worker is working from home or not.

hasFutureSalary.description: Indicates whether a future salary exists.

links.description: The link relations associated with the resource instance.

salaryComponents.description: The salaryComponents resource is the child of the salaries resource and includes all the components that itemize salary adjustments for a given salary record, such as Adjustment, Location, and Merit.

salaryPayRateComponents.title: Salary Rate Components
salaryPayRateComponents.description: The salaryRateComponents resource is the child of the salaries resource and includes all of the rate components for a given salary record. This resource is currently under controlled availability.

salarySimpleComponents.title: Salary Simple Components
salarySimpleComponents.description: The salarySimpleComponents resource is the child of the salaries resource and includes all of the simple components for a given salary record, such as Base Salary, Location Allowance, and Special Allowance.

AdjustmentAmount.title: Adjustment Amount
AdjustmentAmount.description: Change amount for a component used to itemize salary into different reasons.

AdjustmentPercentage.title: Adjustment Percentage
AdjustmentPercentage.description: Change percentage for a component used to itemize salary into different reasons.

ChangeAmountScale.description: Decimal precision of the salary amount, configured in salary basis.

ComponentReasonCode.title: Component
ComponentReasonCode.description: Incremental component code for salary adjustments itemized by reasons, such as merit, location, and cost of living.

SalaryComponentId.description: Unique identifier of the salary component.

PayRateDefinitionId.title: Component
PayRateDefinitionId.description: Unique identifier of the rate definition.

RateAdjustmentAmount.title: Adjustment Amount
RateAdjustmentAmount.description: Change amount for a rate definition.

RateAdjustmentPercent.title: Adjustment Percentage
RateAdjustmentPercent.description: Change percentage for a rate definition.

RateAmount.title: Amount
RateAmount.description: Amount on the rate component of the person's salary.

RateAnnualAmount.title: Annual Amount
RateAnnualAmount.description: Annual value of the rate amount.

RateAnnualFtAmount.title: Annualized Full-Time Amount
RateAnnualFtAmount.description: Annual value of the rate amount, if the worker was working full time.

RateCurrencyCode.title: Currency
RateCurrencyCode.description: Currency code that the rate is stored in, such as USD.

RateDisplaySequence.title: Display Sequence
RateDisplaySequence.description: Display sequence of the rate component.

RateFactor.title: Percentage
RateFactor.description: Percentage on the rate component of the person's salary.

RateMaximumAmount.description: Maximum value allowed for the rate component.

RateMinimumAmount.description: Minimum value required for the rate component.

RatePeriodicityCode.title: Frequency
RatePeriodicityCode.description: Code of the rate periodicity, such as ANNUAL, MONTHLY, or HOURLY.

RateScale.description: Decimal precision of the rate definition.

RateType.description: Type of the rate, such as Primary or Derived.

SalaryPayComponentId.description: Unique identifier of the salary rate component.

AdjustmentAmount.title: Adjustment Amount
AdjustmentAmount.description: Change amount for a simple component.

AdjustmentPercent.title: Adjustment Percentage
AdjustmentPercent.description: Change percentage for a simple component.

Amount.title: Amount
Amount.description: Amount of the person's salary entered on the simple component

AnnualAmount.title: Annual Amount
AnnualAmount.description: Annual value of the simple component amount.

AnnualFtAmount.title: Annualized Full-Time Amount
AnnualFtAmount.description: Annual value of the simple component amount, if the person worked full time.

BasedOnComponentCode.description: Code of the simple component that the percentage applies to.

BasedOnComponentName.description: Name of the simple component that the percentage applies to.

BasisSimpleComponentId.description: Unique identifier of the simple component that the percentage applies to.

ComponentCode.title: Salary Component Code
ComponentCode.description: Code of the salary simple component, such as ORA_BASIC.

ComponentName.title: Component
ComponentName.description: Name of the simple component, which can be configured to accept an amount or percentage.

ComponentType.title: Type
ComponentType.description: Code for the simple component type.

CurrencyCode.title: Currency
CurrencyCode.description: Currency code that the simple component value is stored in, such as USD.

DisplaySequence.title: Display Sequence
DisplaySequence.description: Display sequence of the simple component.

OverallSalaryAffect.title: Overall Salary
OverallSalaryAffect.description: Code for the effect of overall salary.

OverrideGspRate.description: Indicates whether a user can override the value defaulted from the progression ladder.

Percentage.title: Percentage
Percentage.description: Percentage entered for the simple component. This percentage is applied to the specified based on simple component, to calculate the component amount. For example, apply 5% to the Base Salary simplified component.

Scale.description: Decimal precision of the salary amount, configured in salary basis.

SimpleSalaryCompntId.description: Unique identifier of the salary simple component.

UserSelectedComponent.description: Property used to specify whether the incremental components type is SELECTED_COMPONENTS or USER_DECIDES_USAGE.

ActionOccurrenceId.description: Unique identifier of the action occurrence.

ActionReasonId.title: Action Reason
ActionReasonId.description: Unique identifier of the action reason.

AdjustedAnnualFtSalary.description: Annual value of the worker's salary, when working full time per adjusted full-time equivalent.

AdjustedAnnualSalary.description: Annual value of the worker's salary, when working per adjusted full-time equivalent.

AdjustedFTEUsage.title: Adjusted FTE
AdjustedFTEUsage.description: Indicates that adjusted FTE is used.

AdjustedFTEValue.description: Factor that represents the proportion of adjusted full-time hours a person works.

AdjustmentAmount.title: Adjustment Amount
AdjustmentAmount.description: Calculated adjustment amount based on the previous salary.

AdjustmentPercentage.title: Adjustment Percentage
AdjustmentPercentage.description: Calculated adjustment percentage based on the previous salary .

AnnualFullTimeSalary.title: Annualized Full-Time Salary
AnnualFullTimeSalary.description: Annual value of the worker's salary, if working full time.

AnnualSalary.title: Annual Salary
AnnualSalary.description: Annual value of the worker's salary.

AssignmentType.description: Type of assignment, such as an employee, contingent worker, nonworker, or pending worker.

CompaRatio.title: Compa-Ratio
CompaRatio.description: Salary as a percentage of the grade range midpoint. Salary at the midpoint is 100.

CurrencyCode.title: Currency
CurrencyCode.description: Currency code that the salary amount is stored in, such as USD.

FTEValue.title: FTE
FTEValue.description: Factor that represents the proportion of full-time hours a person works.

GradeId.title: Grade
GradeId.description: Unique identifier for the assignment grade.

MultipleComponents.title: Use salary components
MultipleComponents.description: Indicates whether the given salary record has any child records that are either Components, Simple Components, or Rates.

NextSalReviewDate.title: Next Salary Review Date
NextSalReviewDate.description: Date to review a person's assignment salary.

PayrollFactor.description: Payroll multiplier used to convert the salary amount to an annual salary amount, when the frequency is chosen as payroll period.

PayrollFrequencyCode.title: Frequency
PayrollFrequencyCode.description: Code for the payroll frequency when the frequency is chosen as payroll period.

PendingTransactionExists.description: Indicates if a salary change for the person assignment is pending approval.

Quartile.title: Quartile
Quartile.description: Quartile containing the worker's salary. The salary range for the worker's grade is divided into four equal parts called quartiles.

QuartileMeaning.title: Quartile
QuartileMeaning.description: Lookup code meaning of the quartile containing the worker's salary.

Quintile.title: Quintile
Quintile.description: Quintile containing the worker's salary. The salary range for the worker's grade is divided into five equal parts called quintiles.

QuintileMeaning.title: Quintile
QuintileMeaning.description: Lookup code meaning of the quintile containing the worker's salary.

RangePosition.title: Range Position
RangePosition.description: Position of the worker's salary within the assignment grade salary range, expressed as a percentage of 100.

SalaryAmount.title: Salary Amount
SalaryAmount.description: Salary amount on the salary record for the worker assignment.

SalaryAmountScale.description: Decimal precision of the salary amount, configured in salary basis.

SalaryBasisId.description: Unique identifier for the salary basis.

SalaryBasisType.description: Type of the salary basis, such as Salary amount determined by the user or Components.

SalaryFactor.title: Salary Annualization Factor
SalaryFactor.description: Multiplier that converts the salary amount to an annual salary amount. For example, if a worker's base pay is quoted hourly, the annualization factor is 2080 (40 hours per week x 52 weeks).

SalaryFrequencyCode.title: Frequency
SalaryFrequencyCode.description: Code of the salary basis frequency, such as ANNUAL, MONTHLY, HOURLY.

SalaryRangeDefaultValue.description: Value based on the worker assignment grade and grade rate attached with the salary basis.

SalaryRangeMaximum.title: Salary Range Maximum
SalaryRangeMaximum.description: Salary range maximum based on the worker assignment grade.

SalaryRangeMidPoint.title: Salary Range Midpoint
SalaryRangeMidPoint.description: Salary range midpoint based on the worker assignment grade.

SalaryRangeMinimum.title: Salary Range Minimum
SalaryRangeMinimum.description: Salary range minimum based on the worker assignment grade.

SalaryTransactionStatus.description: Indicate whether the salary in cache is new or a correction. The approver scenario in the salary history action uses this information during edits.

AdjustmentPercent.description: Change percentage for a simple component.

Amount.title: Amount
Amount.description: Amount of the person's salary entered on the simple component

AnnualAmount.description: Annual value of the simple component amount.

Metadata-Context.description: If the REST API supports runtime customizations, the shape of the service may change during runtime. The REST client may isolate itself from these changes or choose to interact with the latest version of the API by specifying this header. For example: Metadata-Context:sandbox="TrackEmployeeFeature".

REST-Framework-Version.description: The protocol version between a REST client and service. If the client does not specify this header in the request the server will pick a default version for the API.

salaries-item-post-request.description: The following table describes the body parameters in the request for this task.
